title: ExampleSite / Content / Claims / Claim Merge Without Collapse
summary: exampleSite/content/claims/claim-merge-without-collapse.md is preserved as a raw Hugo source artifact in the XanaNode Hugo substrate.
content:
---
title: "Substrates should merge without collapsing disagreement"
type: "claim"
importance: 5
status: "active"
summary: "Federated substrates should preserve authorship, provenance, and disagreement rather than forcing one merged source of truth."
relationships:
  - type: "claim_of"
    target: "merge-validation"
  - type: "supported_by"
    target: "xananode-core-schema"
  - type: "contradicts"
    target: "single-source-of-truth"
---

The goal of merging substrates is not to erase differences.

The goal is to expose correspondences, conflicts, overlaps, and gaps while preserving who asserted what and why.
